channel.title: NYT > Style
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: BioFluff Is Producing a Faux Fur Made From Plants
item.description: BioFluff, a fashion-tech company based in Paris and New York, is producing a new alternative to animal pelts: one that’s made from plants, not plastics.
item.dc:creator: Chantel Tattoli and Clara Vannucci

item.title: A.I. Slop Videos of Cheating Fruit Are Taking Over TikTok
item.description: A new slop trend on TikTok has taken an analog turn, with fans recreating nonsensical A.I. videos IRL.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:credit: Andrew Bui for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Barrett Washburne.

item.title: Is Taste the One Thing A.I. Can’t Replace?
item.description: Anxious tech workers in Silicon Valley are trying to cultivate a quality they say ChatGPT can’t provide.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Bernstein

item.title: Should I Stop Dyeing My Gray Hair?
item.description: Going gray is a natural part of growing older, but it’s not always obvious when to let nature take its course. Our critic offers tips for doing it in style.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:credit: Dana Golan for The New York Times

item.title: 25 of the Best Looks From Fashion Week Fall 2026
item.description: Members of the Styles staff fantasy-shop the recent collections.
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk
item.media:credit: From left, Dior; Filippo Fior/Gorunway; Simbarashe Cha/The New York Times; Daniele Oberrauch/Gorunway; INDIGITAL.TV

item.title: Best Hair at Fall 2026 Fashion Week in New York, London, Milan & Paris
item.description: Bobs, bangs, buzz cuts — whatever hairstyle you desire, inspiration abounded at recent fashion weeks.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha
item.media:credit: Photographs by Simbarashe Cha/The New York Times

item.title: How Muslim Businesses in New York Prepare to Celebrate Eid
item.description: From Fordham to Bay Ridge to Hollis, Muslim businesses get ready for Eid al-Fitr, the exultant celebration marking the end of the holy month of Ramadan.
item.dc:creator: Sadiba Hasan and Amir Hamja
item.media:credit: Amir Hamja for The New York Times

item.title: Spring Is Here. Do You Care?
item.description: A season celebrated by Shakespeare and the Beatles now ranks behind fall in popularity. After a harsh winter, can it regain its former glory?
item.dc:creator: Steven Kurutz
item.media:credit: Kristian Thacker for The New York Times
item.media:description: Can these little snowdrops compete with maple trees in full autumnal splendor?

item.title: From the Bronx to the Red Carpet, Yulenny Garcia Nails Turn Heads Everywhere
item.description: Whether at hospitals or on red carpets, people with manicures by Yulenny Garcia, a nail technician in the Bronx, turn heads.
item.dc:creator: Sandra E. Garcia and Josefina Santos
item.media:credit: Josefina Santos for The New York Times

item.title: Leon Radvinsky, 43, Dies; Built the Adult-Entertainment Giant OnlyFans
item.description: By leveraging social media and the influencer economy, he turned his website into a byword for online pornography in the 21st century.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

item.title: Tiny Love Stories: ‘Six Years Younger and 30 Pounds Lighter’
item.description: Modern Love in miniature, featuring reader-submitted stories of no more than 100 words.
item.media:credit: Brian Rea

item.title: What Is ‘One-Sided Monogamy’?
item.description: “Inside the Manosphere,” a new Netflix documentary, has fueled discussions about the relationship style.
item.dc:creator: Gina Cherelus
item.media:credit: Netflix
item.media:description: In a recent documentary by Louis Theroux, left, the manosphere influencer Justin Waller, right, describes his relationship as “one-sided monogamy.”

item.title: What to Know About the ‘Laguna Beach’ Reunion
item.description: The show, which depicted the lives of a group of wealthy high schoolers and helped to pioneer a genre of soap-style reality TV, will return in April with a reunion special.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Roy

item.title: The Good List: 8 Things to Add Delight to Your Day
item.description: March Madness madness, better small talk and the morality of sandwiches.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch
item.media:credit: Illustration by John Gall. Flowers: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times

item.title: Daphne Selfe, World’s Oldest Professional Model, Dies at 97
item.description: Starting in 1949, she had an on-and-off career that lasted for 75 years. But her biggest success came late in life, modeling for labels like Dolce & Gabbana.
item.dc:creator: Bonnie Wertheim
item.media:credit: Visual China Group, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Daphne Selfe in 2010. Her skin-care and diet regimen, she said, included “Nivea, broccoli and the odd glass of champagne.”

item.title: Our Youngest Child Has Cut Ties With Our Family. Help!
item.description: A reader is distraught over the rift with her 37-year-old child, who is insisting on having a neutral party present for any potential meeting with the rest of the family.
item.dc:creator: Philip Galanes
item.media:credit: Miguel Porlan

item.title: Street Style Look of the Week: Wearing Blue and Black
item.description: Cobalt-blue knitwear distinguished an ensemble from others outside a coffee shop in Tokyo.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha

item.title: What Happened With Chappell Roan, a Security Guard and Jude Law’s Daughter?
item.description: The singer has denied the allegations of the encounter, which took place in Brazil and had kicked up hot debate online.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:credit: Simbarashe Cha/The New York Times
item.media:description: The singer Chappell Roan is at the center of an internet drama set over breakfast at a hotel in São Paulo, Brazil.

item.title: What’s It Like to Be Back in Print After 20 Years? A Bit Odd.
item.description: Nancy Lemann published her first novel at 28. Then came “the doom.” Now she’s back in the spotlight, and not exactly comfortable with it.
item.dc:creator: Marie Solis
item.media:credit: Annie Flanagan for The New York Times
item.media:description: The author outside the apartment in New Orleans where she wrote “Lives of the Saints.”

item.title: In “Group: The Schopenhauer Effect,” Filmmaker Simulates Group Therapy
item.description: Alexis Lloyd, the son of a psychoanalyst, made a group therapy film that pulled him into a vortex of endless group therapy.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Hess
item.media:credit: Timothy O'Connell for The New York Times

item.title: Should I Tell My Employee I Think He Has ADHD?
item.description: Plus, my co-workers had an affair and misused company funds. Somehow, I’m the one dealing with the fallout.
item.dc:creator: Max Read
item.media:credit: Photo illustration by Margeaux Walter for The New York Times

item.title: A Guardian of the Past Takes His Treasures Online
item.description: Louis Bofferding has an intimate feel for rare antiques with dramatic back stories. But his wealthy clients are content to point and click.
item.dc:creator: Guy Trebay
item.media:credit: Tony Cenicola/The New York Times
item.media:description: Louis Bofferding at his soon-to-close shop on the Upper East Side of Manhattan. “Objects have voices and personalities,” he said.

item.title: What BTS Wore for Its Comeback Concert and Why
item.description: The band’s looks, by the Korean brand Songzio, represented a bigger story about Korean culture and meaning.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

item.title: Elsa Schiaparelli Exhibit Shares How She Dressed a City in Couture
item.description: The Parisian couturier is best remembered for her outlandish designs. When she opened shop in buttoned-up London, she didn’t hold back.
item.dc:creator: Louis Lucero II

item.title: Influencers Caught in ‘Bachelorette’ Fallout After ABC Pulls New Season
item.description: When ABC abruptly pulled a planned season of “The Bachelorette,” some whose livelihoods are wrapped up in the series were thrown for a loop.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher

item.title: Tunisia’s Capital Is Captivating a New Generation of Travelers
item.description: Almost 15 years since the revolution in Tunisia, its capital is attracting visitors who want to be part of a thrilling, but fragile, creative blossoming.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Donohue and Jeremie Souteyrat
item.media:description: Tunis blends a Mediterranean climate, North African culture and vibrant art, fashion, food and design scenes.

item.title: ‘The Alchemist’ Brought Them Together
item.description: But a pandemic breakup became a turning point for Arianna Davis and Jay Santana. He came to appreciate their own love story.
item.dc:creator: Valeriya Safronova

item.title: ‘I’m Dead. Don’t Send Me Any More Mail.’
item.description: My relationship with my landlord was among the most reliable of my life. I miss her.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Uhler

item.title: He Cooked Up a Surprise Proposal in Mexico
item.description: In 2021, Noah Michelson and Benji Jones began a relatively old-fashioned courtship with FaceTime, walks and waiting.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Block

item.title: A Facebook Friend Request Leads to a Caribbean Connection
item.description: Romario Ricketts, from Jamaica, was moved to reach out to Greshawna Clement, from Guyana, after seeing her at a student demonstration in 2018 and “admired the passion with which she was speaking.”
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce

item.title: She Calls Him ‘Peter Planner’
item.description: Peter Plante is always planning ahead, including making a secret dinner reservation on his first date with Jill Barry. So, she gave him a nickname.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky

item.title: Thomas Gentille, Artist Who Made Wearable Sculpture, Dies at 89
item.description: He was a master jeweler, but his pieces looked more like miniature contemporary artworks than anything you’d find at Cartier.
item.dc:creator: Penelope Green
item.media:credit: Jack Manning/The New York Times
item.media:description: Thomas Gentille in 1980. “I think you can express everything in jewelry,” he said. “And that’s what I’m trying to do.”

item.title: Timothée Chalamet Loses Favor With Fans — Even Club Chalamet — After Oscars
item.description: After a rocky Oscars campaign, the actor may have lost favor with parts of his fervent fan base, including the popular social media account Club Chalamet.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi
item.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times
item.media:description: Timothée Chalamet’s promotional tour for “Marty Supreme,” for which he was nominated for the Oscar for best actor, was divisive among some fans.

item.title: Doctors’ Tearful Match Week Videos Are TikTok’s Hottest Drama
item.description: Match Week videos, in which early-career doctors film themselves receiving their residency assignments, scratch a very real parasocial itch.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher

item.title: Baby Carrots and Espresso Martinis: How New Yorkers Prepared for a 5-Hour Opera
item.description: Audiences packed the house for a new production of “Tristan und Isolde.” They also came prepared, with (at least) five different forms of coffee.
item.dc:creator: Louis Lucero II
item.media:credit: Sara Krulwich/The New York Times
item.media:description: Michael Spyres, center, as Tristan in the Metropolitan Opera’s new production of “Tristan und Isolde.”

item.title: ‘Marc by Sofia’ Review: Coppola Profiles Her Friend Jacobs
item.description: Sofia Coppola profiles the fashion designer Marc Jacobs, but the documentary strains to arrive at even the most basic revelations.
item.dc:creator: Natalia Winkelman

item.title: Why Vintage Photo Booths Are the New Engagement Shoot
item.description: Vintage photo strips are becoming a new way couples share their engagements, offering a spontaneous, low-cost alternative to formal shoots.
item.dc:creator: Gina Cherelus
